key_insight: Agentic Symbolic Search (ASYS) turns PDE theory and prior knowledge into differentiable symbolic programs, then refines their forms and parameters to recover or approximate interpretable PDE laws.
engineering_relevance: The method could give engineers and scientists compact, human-readable PDE models that are easier to analyze, validate, and generalize than mesh outputs or black-box neural predictions.
method: Agent uses PDE theory, problem constraints, and past search experience to propose symbolic candidates.
Evolutionary search refines the symbolic structure while gradient-based optimization fits continuous parameters.
Produces analytical expressions or approximations instead of only numerical tables or neural surrogates.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on five PDE problems, spanning bounded dynamics, finite-time blow-up, and free-boundary focusing, including previously unavailable interpretable formulas such as an Allen-Cahn interface law and a Keller-Segel contraction law.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of prior guidance and symbolic search space design, so strong domain input and careful implementation are likely necessary.
paper_title: Agentic Symbolic Search: Characterizing PDEs Beyond Hand-crafted Expressions, Meshes, and Neural Networks
